body:
### Describe the bug

If you make a direct call to a non-final member it will fail due the cython do not expose the methods headers required for it, it does not even produces an error, it just generates the code pretending everything ok and fails when compiling c or c++.

It could work if cython do not perform optimizations but tbh it would make cython useless, if a simple call like this can not be correctly coded with a simple direct c-call. 



### Code to reproduce the behaviour:

```cython
PYTHON setup.py build_ext --inplace
PYTHON -c "import b; import a"

######## setup.py ########

from Cython.Build.Dependencies import cythonize
import Cython.Compiler.Options
Cython.Compiler.Options.cimport_from_pyx = True

from distutils.core import setup

setup(
  ext_modules = cythonize("*.pyx"),
)

######## a.pyx ########
from b import B
from b cimport B
from cython import ccall, cclass

@cclass
class A(B):
    @ccall
    def s(self):
        B.mtd(self)

######## b.pyx ########

from cython import cclass, ccall

@cclass
class B:
    @ccall
    def mtd(self):
        pass

```


### Expected behaviour

Cython should generate headers for all the `__pyx_f_...` symbols so the modules can be linked without issues.
Currently it is only happening for final methods.
Or at last resort provide a flag to produce module isolated (which default this to slow python method call).

### OS

_No response_

### Python version

_No response_

### Cython version

_No response_

### Additional context

The error looks like this:

```
-- stdout:Compiling a.pyx because it changed.
Compiling b.pyx because it changed.
[1/2] Cythonizing a.pyx
[2/2] Cythonizing b.pyx

-- stderr:a.c:2773:15: error: call to undeclared function '__pyx_f_1b_1B_mtd'; ISO C99 and later do not support implicit function declarations [-Wimplicit-function-declaration]
 2773 |   __pyx_t_1 = __pyx_f_1b_1B_mtd(((struct __pyx_obj_1b_B *)__pyx_v_self), 1); if (unlikely(!__pyx_t_1)) __PYX_ERR(0, 9, __pyx_L1_error)
      |               ^
a.c:2773:15: note: did you mean '__pyx_f_1a_1A_s'?
a.c:2694:18: note: '__pyx_f_1a_1A_s' declared here
 2694 | static PyObject *__pyx_f_1a_1A_s(struct __pyx_obj_1a_A *__pyx_v_self, int __pyx_skip_dispatch) {
      |                  ^
a.c:2773:13: error: incompatible integer to pointer conversion assigning to 'PyObject *' (aka 'struct _object *') from 'int' [-Wint-conversion]
 2773 |   __pyx_t_1 = __pyx_f_1b_1B_mtd(((struct __pyx_obj_1b_B *)__pyx_v_self), 1); if (unlikely(!__pyx_t_1)) __PYX_ERR(0, 9, __pyx_L1_error)
      |             ^ ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
2 errors generated.
error: command '/usr/bin/clang' failed with exit code 1

```
title: [BUG] Direct call to non-final method do not work when cross modules
